_comment: Crosswalk from governance frameworks to the checks K8sMatrixWarden already runs. Each requirement maps to CIS Kubernetes Benchmark v1.8 control ids (evaluated by the CIS engine) and/or OWASP Kubernetes Top 10 codes (carried on findings). This is the KUBERNETES-RELEVANT SUBSET of each framework, not the whole standard — requirements a cluster scan cannot speak to are deliberately absent, never silently passed. Every 'cis' id here must exist in frameworks/cis_catalog.CIS_1_8 and every 'owasp' code in taxonomy/owasp_k8s_top10.json (enforced by tests/test_compliance.py).

PCI-DSS-4.0.name: PCI DSS v4.0
PCI-DSS-4.0.scope_note: Kubernetes-relevant subset. Full PCI DSS attestation requires assessment beyond cluster posture (physical, personnel, network segmentation of the CDE).

requirements.title: Network security controls between trusted and untrusted networks
Inbound traffic to the cardholder data environment restricted
Stored account data not directly accessible from untrusted networks
Only necessary services, protocols and functions enabled
Stored account data rendered unreadable (encryption at rest)
Strong cryptography protects account data in transit
Access assigned based on least privilege
System and application accounts and access managed
Users and administrators identified with a unique ID
Strong authentication for users and administrators
Audit logs enabled and active for all system components
Audit log files protected from modification
Audit logs reviewed to identify anomalies

SOC2.name: SOC 2 (Trust Services Criteria 2017, rev. 2022)
SOC2.scope_note: Kubernetes-relevant Common Criteria. A SOC 2 report also covers organizational, HR and vendor controls outside cluster posture.

requirements.title: Logical access security software and infrastructure restrict access
User registration and authorization before access is granted
Access based on roles and responsibilities (least privilege)
Logical access boundary protection against external threats
Transmission of data restricted and protected
Controls prevent or detect unauthorized or malicious software
Detection of configuration changes and vulnerabilities
Security events evaluated to determine response
Changes to infrastructure authorized and controlled

ISO-27001-2022.name: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A
ISO-27001-2022.scope_note: Kubernetes-relevant Annex A controls. The full ISMS certification covers policy, risk management and organizational controls beyond a cluster scan.

NIST-800-53-r5.name: NIST SP 800-53 Revision 5
NIST-800-53-r5.scope_note: Kubernetes-relevant control subset. A full 800-53 / FedRAMP assessment spans hundreds of controls across physical, personnel and organizational families.
